mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over rolling 3-year windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals severe economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, which sustains corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a t-statistic above 1.5 over any 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-crisis recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases produce negative or flat 20-day SPX returns during recovery periods, with a cumulative alpha below -0.5 percent per month.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate strengthening labor demand, which raises household income expectations and drives immediate positive S&P 500 price adjustments over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day SPX direction, with a hit rate indistinguishable from random chance over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 2.5 percent year-over-year, inflationary pressures force monetary authorities to tighten policy, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 60 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and real GDP confirms broad-based economic expansion, reducing recession risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and GDPC1 growth rates yields a negative coefficient in out-of-sample regression models predicting 20-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real personal consumption expenditures during a liquidity trap regime signal collapsing consumer demand, which forces corporate earnings downgrades and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines produce positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50 percent of observations during low-rate environments, invalidating the demand contraction channel.
